categories.description: Requests that are action needed for the task automation team.

parameters.text: =✅ Distill documents with Google Drive and Telegram at scale — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### On Telegram Event

Native push trigger — telegram calls this workflow the moment the event happens (no polling).

Next: Keep Valid Requests

🔑 Telegram bot token

### Keep Valid Requests

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Classify with AI

### Classify with AI

LLM classification into: Action Needed, Reference, Ignore (+fallback). Output port order matches this list.

Ports: ①→ Store as Action Needed · ②→ Store as Reference, Capture Failure Context · ③→ Store as Ignore · ④→ Alert Telegram — Other

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Classify with AI** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Store as Action Needed

Appends a new row to 'Request Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Store as Reference

Appends a new row to 'Request Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Store as Ignore

Appends a new row to 'Request Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Alert Telegram — Other

Sends a Telegram message to the configured chat — the same bot can also receive replies, so this doubles as a two-way ops channel.

Ports: ①→ Reunite Branches · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Telegram bot token

### Reunite Branches

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per request (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Copy to Google Drive

### Archive Copy to Google Drive

Uploads the generated file to Google Drive as a timestamped archive copy.

Ports: ①→ Enrich with Reference Data · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Archive Copy to Google Drive

parameters.content:
### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Request Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ai-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#ai-ops`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Request Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Distill documents with Google Drive and Telegram at scale

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Distill documents with Google Drive and Telegram at scale. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual task automation is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
On Telegram Event → Keep Valid Requests → Classify with AI → Store as Action Needed → Reunite Branches → Fingerprint for Idempotency → Drop Already-Processed Items → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating task automation in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Improves response time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Groq API key; Slack OAuth2; Telegram bot token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

One Text Classifier node replaces what would otherwise be a chain of brittle IF/Switch keyword checks — the LLM handles the ambiguous cases a rules engine can't. Each category gets its own action node, so a failure in one branch (say, a bad Slack call) never blocks the others; Merge reunites them so the run still reports one clean completion.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Add a 4th category with its own action branch.
2. Replace one action (e.g. a Slack ping) with a task-creation node and see how the wiring changes.
3. Feed the classifier deliberately ambiguous text and check which fallback category it lands in.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your task automation process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
